description: Brute force login attempt detected — 47 failed attempts from 198.51.100.23 against user admin@atlascloud.io
resolution: IP blocked via WAF rule; user account locked and password reset initiated

description: Security group modification — ingress rule added allowing 0.0.0.0/0 on port 8080 in staging VPC
resolution: Unauthorized SG change reverted; developer contacted — was testing and forgot to scope to VPN CIDR

description: Unusual data export — api-key user svc-reporting exported 2.3GB via customer-data-api in 15 minutes (baseline: 200MB/hour)
resolution: Investigated — legitimate bulk report generation for enterprise customer quarterly review. Alert threshold adjusted for this service account.

description: TLS certificate expiring within 30 days — reporting-engine.internal.atlascloud.net
resolution: Certificate renewal ticket created (SEC-4498). Renewed October 7.

description: Kubernetes pod created with privileged security context in production namespace
resolution: Developer deployed with incorrect pod security policy. Pod terminated and redeployed with restricted context.

description: Port scan detected from internal IP 10.20.3.45 targeting payment-processor subnet
resolution: Traced to vulnerability scanner (Qualys) running scheduled scan. Alert tuned to allowlist Qualys scanner IPs.

description: Impossible travel — user j.martinez@atlascloud.io authenticated from New York (10:55 EDT) and Singapore (16:55 SGT) within 3 minutes
resolution: VPN session from Singapore confirmed as legitimate — user was using VPN to Singapore office. Resolved as false positive.

description: CrowdStrike Falcon detection — suspicious PowerShell execution on endpoint DEV-WS-034 (user: r.chang)
resolution: Endpoint isolated. Investigation confirmed benign developer script for local testing. CrowdStrike exclusion added for approved dev tools.

description: Elevated database query volume — 340% above baseline on customer-data-api RDS instance
resolution: Traced to new feature deployment causing N+1 query issue. Development team notified. Query optimized in hotfix.

description: New EC2 instance launched in production account outside of approved IaC pipeline
resolution: Instance launched by SRE for emergency debugging during latency incident. Instance terminated after use. SRE reminded of IaC-only policy.

description: S3 bucket versioning disabled on atlas-cloud-staging-assets
resolution: Staging bucket — versioning re-enabled. Not a production asset.

description: Service account svc-data-pipeline credentials used from unexpected IP 203.0.113.88 (not in allowlist)
resolution: Investigated — IP belongs to new NAT gateway deployed October 16. Allowlist updated.

description: Lateral movement pattern detected — 5 internal hosts contacted within 2 minutes from 10.20.5.12
resolution: Source was health check service performing connectivity validation. Alert tuned.

description: DNS tunneling indicators detected — high-entropy DNS queries to suspicious domain from 10.20.3.78
resolution: Endpoint quarantined. Investigation found no malicious activity — DNS queries were from a legitimate SaaS analytics SDK. Domain allowlisted after vendor verification.

description: IAM user access key older than 90 days — svc-legacy-reports
resolution: Key rotated. Automated rotation configured via Vault.

description: sudo access granted to non-admin user on bastion host
resolution: Temporary sudo for emergency maintenance. Access revoked after 2 hours. Break-glass procedure documented.

description: WAF rule disabled in production — rule ID WAF-OWASP-SQLi-001
resolution: Rule temporarily disabled by SRE due to false positive blocking legitimate traffic. Rule re-enabled with refined pattern after 45 minutes.

description: Credential stuffing attack — 12000 login attempts in 10 minutes from botnet (multiple IPs)
resolution: Rate limiting engaged automatically. Attacker IPs blocked. No successful authentications. WAF bot protection rules updated.

description: Unencrypted EBS volume detected — vol-0a1b2c3d4e5f (attached to staging instance)
resolution: Staging volume — encrypted and replaced. SCP updated to prevent future unencrypted volume creation.

description: Outbound connection to known C2 IP 198.51.100.187 from 10.20.4.22
resolution: Endpoint isolated. Full forensic analysis. Connection was DNS resolution for a domain that recently rotated to a known-bad IP. No malicious activity confirmed. CrowdStrike scan clean.

description: Database admin query executed outside maintenance window — DELETE on customer_events table
resolution: DBA performed data cleanup per approved JIRA ticket. Maintenance window requirement reminder sent to DBA team.

description: AWS S3 GetObject spike — 4500 requests in 5 minutes from IAM role arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/LambdaDataProcessor targeting atlas-cloud-customer-docs bucket
resolution: Lambda function processing large batch job. Confirmed legitimate. Alert threshold adjusted for batch processing hours.

description: CloudTrail logging paused for 8 minutes in us-east-1 due to S3 bucket policy misconfiguration
resolution: S3 bucket policy corrected. CloudTrail logs verified — no gap in coverage (buffered and delivered after policy fix).

description: Okta admin role assigned to user not in authorized admin group — user: temp-contractor-0847
resolution: Role assignment reversed within 30 minutes. HR confirmed contractor was supposed to have limited read-only access. Okta admin provisioning workflow tightened.

description: New RDS instance created in production account — not matching any approved Terraform plan
resolution: Instance created by Platform team for database migration testing. Should have used staging. Instance terminated.

description: CrowdStrike detection — Cobalt Strike beacon indicators on endpoint SRE-WS-012 (user: k.nakamura)
resolution: Endpoint immediately isolated. Full forensic investigation. Determined to be authorized red team exercise — Security team was conducting internal testing but failed to notify SOC. Process updated to require SOC pre-notification for red team activities.

description: Unusual outbound traffic volume from ml-inference-engine — 15GB in 1 hour (baseline: 2GB/hour)
resolution: ML model retraining job generating large output dataset. Confirmed legitimate. Alert baseline updated for retraining windows.

description: Multiple failed MFA challenges for user d.owens@atlascloud.io (CTO) — 5 failures in 3 minutes
resolution: User confirmed hardware token battery was dying. Token replaced. No unauthorized access.

description: Bulk customer PII export — 50000 records exported via customer-data-api by svc-support-tool
resolution: Support tool performing authorized bulk export for enterprise customer data migration. Export was pre-approved (JIRA SUP-8934). Alert rule updated to check against pre-approved export tickets.

description: Kubernetes pod running as root in production — search-indexer pod search-indexer-7b9f8c-x4kml
resolution: Pod security context corrected in Helm chart. Redeployed with non-root user. PSP violation indicates Kyverno policy was not enforcing on this namespace.

description: Distributed brute force — 8500 attempts against /api/v2/auth/login from 340 unique IPs
resolution: Automated bot protection engaged. Geographic rate limiting applied. No successful authentications.

description: AWS Config rule non-compliant — 3 EBS volumes without encryption in production
resolution: Volumes attached to newly provisioned instances created via CLI (bypassed Terraform). Volumes encrypted in place. CLI deployment blocked via SCP.

description: Large file upload to external IP — 3.2GB uploaded from 10.20.5.33 to 104.248.67.12 via HTTPS
resolution: Investigated — automated backup to authorized off-site storage (Backblaze B2). IP verified as Backblaze endpoint. Alert allowlist updated.

description: Production EKS cluster scaling event — 47 new pods created in 2 minutes (normal: 2-3 pods/hour)
resolution: Auto-scaler responded to sudden traffic spike (Black Friday overflow). Confirmed legitimate scaling behavior.

description: Unusual database schema modification in production — ALTER TABLE on customer_accounts
resolution: Approved schema migration per release notes v4.2.1. DBA confirmed migration was in the approved change window.

description: AWS IAM role assumption from unexpected account — arn:aws:iam::987654321098:role/CrossAccountAudit
resolution: Role assumption from auditor's AWS account. Confirmed authorized — SOC 2 auditor performing evidence collection. Role has read-only access.

description: Exposed credential detected — API key found in public GitHub repository (atlas-cloud/frontend-sdk commit abc1234)
resolution: API key immediately revoked. Key was a test/staging key with no production access. Developer counseled. Pre-commit hook for secret detection deployed to repository.

description: DNS query volume spike — 450% increase in DNS queries from data-pipeline service
resolution: New data source integration causing high DNS resolution volume. Caching DNS resolver deployed to reduce query volume.

description: Database query returning >100K rows from customer_accounts table by user svc-analytics
resolution: Analytics service running quarterly aggregation job. Pre-approved in change calendar. Alert updated to recognize scheduled analytics windows.

description: CrowdStrike detection — suspicious DLL side-loading on endpoint FINANCE-WS-003 (user: m.johnson)
resolution: Endpoint isolated. Full scan performed. DLL was legitimate third-party finance software update. CrowdStrike exclusion added after vendor verification.

description: Production database failover triggered — RDS Multi-AZ failover event on atlas-prod-primary
resolution: Automated failover due to underlying hardware issue on primary. Failover completed in 37 seconds. No data loss. AWS support case opened.

description: Application error rate spike — 34% error rate on customer-data-api (baseline: 0.1%)
resolution: Already investigating per ALT-Q4-041. Error rate caused by database failover. Connections re-established within 90 seconds. Error rate returned to baseline by 08:55.

description: Authentication latency spike — p99 latency 4200ms (baseline: 180ms) on user-auth-service
resolution: Already investigating per ALT-Q4-041. Auth latency caused by database failover cascading to auth service. Resolved when database connections stabilized.
notes: Related to INC-2025-Q4-041. Acknowledged before alert generated (team already engaged).

description: Outbound connection to Tor exit node from 10.20.6.15
resolution: Endpoint quarantined. Investigation determined developer was testing Tor connectivity for a customer feature request. No malicious activity. Developer reminded of acceptable use policy. **SLA missed by 4 minutes — team was occupied with ongoing incident INC-2025-Q4-041 post-mortem meeting.**

description: Privileged database access outside maintenance window — DBA direct connection to atlas-prod-primary from 10.50.2.8
resolution: DBA was performing emergency index rebuild due to query performance degradation affecting customers. Approved verbally by CTO (Derek Owens) at 03:30. Written approval documented post-hoc at 09:00. **SLA missed by 10 minutes — on-call engineer was handling simultaneous page for eu-west-1 latency issue.**
notes: INC-2025-Q4-045. SLA breach — 25 minutes vs 15-minute target. On-call was handling concurrent alert.
